title: Disintermediation Defense

content.statement: If users can bypass you, they eventually will.
content.question: What stops two sides of your marketplace from just bypassing your app after meeting once?
content.equation: DD = (Platform Value + Lock-In + Enforcement + Switching Cost) ÷ Bypass Incentive
sop: Identify exactly where in your process two parties could exchange contact info and go direct.
Rate your Platform Value 1-10, what you provide beyond the initial connection (payments, trust, tools, discovery) (see calculation, page 6).
Rate your Lock-In and Enforcement 1-10, how much structural or policy friction exists against going around you (see calculation, page 6).
Rate your Switching Cost 1-10, how much a party loses by leaving your platform (see calculation, page 6).
Rate the Bypass Incentive 1-10, how much money or hassle a party saves by going direct (see calculation, page 6), then add one ongoing service that only makes sense while staying on-platform.
content.explainer: Connection alone isn't defensible. Ongoing value is. Build reasons to stay in the app.
questions: What stops two sides of your marketplace from just bypassing your app after meeting once?
If your two sides met through you once, is there any reason for them to come back through you a second time?
What do you provide after the introduction that a direct, off-platform relationship couldn't replicate?

definitions.def: When two parties on a platform connect once, then transact directly off-platform forever after.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Platform Value 7, Lock-In 5, Enforcement 4, Switching Cost 6. 7 plus 5 plus 4 plus 6 equals 22, divided by Bypass Incentive 6 equals 3.7. Higher means parties have real reasons to keep transacting through you. Lower means the moment they meet, they have every incentive to cut you out.
content.closing_statement: Ongoing value is what keeps people from cutting you out.

caption:
Principle Nº 80: Disintermediation Defense

If users can bypass you, they eventually will. Connection alone isn't defensible.

Ask yourself: What stops two sides of your marketplace from just bypassing your app after meeting once?
